page: Microsoft Azure E2bs_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EBSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture is supported by hyper-threading, providing 2 threads per core. Cache specifications include 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external block storage.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1890.78 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1623.0, both falling into average tiers. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression at 28.45 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 10.27 GB/sec also demonstrate average performance. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show significant limitations. The multi-core Geekbench score of 1880.0 and stress-ng multi-core score of 1932.89 are categorized as poor. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached large-block read and write speeds measuring 15.07 GB/sec and 13.81 GB/sec respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E2bs_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on dedicated hardware, making it a viable entry-point option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. However, users face a performance tradeoff: while single-threaded execution is stable, multi-threaded scaling and memory bandwidth are weak. This is reflected in poor database operations and a low Redis SET throughput of 1.98 million operations per second. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight memory-intensive applications, small-scale caching, basic text processing, and development environments rather than high-throughput databases or parallelized compute tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E2bs_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EBSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage for external block storage. Benchmarks indicate average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1623.0 and stable single-threaded decompression. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are limited, as evidenced by a weak Passmark CPU score of 2986.72 and a poor multi-core Geekbench score of 1880.0. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-bound workloads with low parallel processing needs. It is best suited for small-scale caching, lightweight databases, text processing, and general-purpose development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2bs_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It offers stable single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2bs_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, designed for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads requiring dedicated Intel Xeon processing.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per core ratio.
No local storage or GPU, but supports Premium Storage for external block storage.
Average single-core performance paired with limited multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling.
Best suited for lightweight databases, small-scale caching, and development environments.
